name: Sóller, Muleta, and Cap Gros Lighthouse Loop
name_original: Faro des Cap Gros — circular desde Sóller por la sierra de Muleta — Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana
description: This difficult 11.4-mile mountain biking route from Sóller to Faro des Cap Gros offers panoramic views of the Mallorcan coast and Tramuntana
region_intro: Mountain biking around Sóller offers diverse terrain within the Serra de Tramuntana mountain range, a UNESCO World Heritage site. The region features steep hills, green valleys, and rugged cliffs, providing varied landscapes for mountain bike trails. Riders can explore lush orange and lemon groves in the Sóller Valley, as well as coastal paths with sea views. This blend of mountains and sea creates a dynamic environment for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Sóller The most popular mountain bike trail is Sóller, Muleta, and Cap Gros Lighthouse Loop, a 11.4 miles (18.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views of the coastline and the Cap Gros Lighthouse. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Port de Sóller Promenade – Deià loop from Port de Sóller, a difficult 26.6 miles (42.8 km) path. This extensive loop connects the Port de Sóller promenade with the scenic village of Deià, traversing varied mountain and coastal terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Port de Sóller – Spain Square, Fornalutx loop from Torre Picada, a 17.9 miles (28.9 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes and charming villages, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Mountain biking around Sóller is defined by mountainous terrain, coastal paths, and green valleys. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Sóller are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1,200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sóller's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Sóller?
A: There are nearly 30 mountain bike trails around Sóller, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 3 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 14 difficult routes, all within the stunning Serra de Tramuntana mountain range.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Sóller?
A: The terrain around Sóller is incredibly diverse. You'll encounter mountainous landscapes with steep hills and green valleys, lush orange and lemon groves in the Sóller Valley, and rugged coastal paths offering spectacular sea views. Trails range from rocky singletracks to 4-wheel drive paths, providing a dynamic riding experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Sóller?
A: Yes, Sóller offers a few easier routes suitable for families. While many trails are moderate to difficult, there are 3 easy routes available. These typically traverse less challenging terrain, often through the picturesque Sóller Valley, allowing for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Sóller?
A: While many trails in Sóller are suitable for dogs, especially those on wider paths or less technical terrain, it's always best to check specific route details on komoot. Some areas within the Serra de Tramuntana might have restrictions or challenging sections that are not ideal for pets. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and on a leash where required.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I might see while mountain biking in Sóller?
A: Sóller's trails offer breathtaking views and pass by many significant landmarks. You could cycle past the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/253414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pass of Honor&lt;/a&gt;, or experience the dramatic scenery near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/324215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de Biniaraix Stone Path&lt;/a&gt;. Coastal routes often provide glimpses of the Cap Gros Lighthouse, while inland trails offer views of Puig Major, Mallorca's highest mountain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Sóller?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Sóller are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010384586/soller-muleta-and-cap-gros-lighthouse-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sóller, Muleta, and Cap Gros Lighthouse Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic coastal views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38266799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Port de Sóller – Port de Sóller Promenade loop from Sóller&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate route that takes you through varied landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Sóller?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Sóller is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making longer rides challenging, while winter can bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain, though many trails remain rideable.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Sóller?
A: The mountain bike trails around Sóller are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 200 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain climbs to scenic coastal paths, and the stunning views of the Serra de Tramuntana and the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there options for advanced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: Absolutely. Sóller is known for its challenging terrain, with 14 difficult routes available. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1463019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Sóller Promenade – Deià loop from Port de Sóller&lt;/a&gt; offer extensive distances and significant elevation gains, traversing varied mountain and coastal landscapes that will test experienced riders.

Q: Where can I find cafes or amenities near the mountain bike trails in Sóller?
A: Sóller town and Port de Sóller offer numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and relax. Many trails start or pass through these areas, or charming villages like Fornalutx, making it easy to find amenities. You can also find bike rental services and guided tours in Sóller, such as Tramuntana Tours.

Q: Is public transport available to access mountain biking areas in Sóller?
A: Sóller has good public transport connections that can help you access various areas. The vintage Sóller tram connects the town to Port de Sóller, and a historic train links Sóller to Palma, traversing scenic mountain routes. These can be useful for reaching starting points or returning after a ride, though checking bike transport policies is advisable.

Q: Are there any specific mountain passes I might encounter on the trails?
A: Yes, the Serra de Tramuntana region around Sóller is home to several notable mountain passes. You might find yourself cycling near the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/103087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll dels Reis (682 m)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/228373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tofla Pass&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular with both mountain and road cyclists for their challenging climbs and rewarding views.
tips: In the middle of the Sierra de Tramuntana is hidden this wonderful town with its quiet promenade known for its tram that runs along the sea. In Port de Sóller you can also enjoy its beaches, as well as taste the local gastronomy with its many restaurants.

With a height of 22 meters, located 120 meters above sea level in the Morro des Xoriguer, is the Cap Gros lighthouse.

An ideal place to enjoy the views of the Mallorcan coast, as well as the admirable Sierra de Tramuntana.
An ideal location to contemplate and enjoy the views of the bay of Puerto de Sóller and the mountains of the Sierra de Tramuntana that surround this beautiful place.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Faro des Cap Gros mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as a difficult mountain bike ride. It requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. Be prepared for challenging sections where you might need to dismount and push your bike, especially on unpaved or singletrack segments.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse mix of surfaces. You'll encounter asphalt, unpaved sections, natural paths, paved roads, and gravel. There are also segments of road, street, path, and singletrack. The road leading to the Cap Gros Lighthouse is notably winding and steep.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect breathtaking panoramic views throughout the ride. Key highlights include the stunning bay of Port de Sóller Promenade, the historic Cap Gros Lighthouse itself, and the majestic Serra de Tramuntana mountains. The route offers ideal vantage points for admiring the Mallorcan coastline, ancient olive groves, and the deep blue Mediterranean Sea. Don't miss the Nice views of the bay of the port of Sóller.

faqs.question: Is the Faro des Cap Gros mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact mountain bike trail are not detailed, the route passes through the Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana. Generally, dogs are permitted in natural parks in Mallorca but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these seasons, the weather is milder, making for more comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, and some winter days might be too cold or wet for an enjoyable ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need specific permits or to pay entrance fees to access this mountain bike trail. It is publicly accessible, traversing through the Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Faro des Cap Gros mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Sóller, making it convenient for parking. You can typically find parking in Port de Sóller, which serves as a good starting point for this circular route. Be aware that parking can be busy, especially during peak season, so arriving early is advisable.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other well-known routes in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it includes sections of the GR 221 Dry Stone Route (both Stage 4: Can Boi - Muleta and Stage 5: Muleta - Tossals verds), as well as parts of the El Camí IT4TR4 El Camí Illenc: de l'Alguer a Formentera and the Port de Sóller - Sóller trail. You'll also encounter segments of the Camí des Rost and various other local camins.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sóller and Port de Sóller offer a variety of cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Near the Cap Gros Lighthouse, you'll find the Refugi de Muleta, a secluded hilltop hostel that was once a military telegraph station. It offers a terrace with sea views and can be a good stopover for refreshments or an overnight stay.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and diverse terrain, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate mountain biking gear. Sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses) is essential, especially during sunnier months. A helmet is mandatory, and consider knee/elbow pads for added safety. A fully charged phone and a navigation device with the route loaded are also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Is there a specific direction recommended for riding this circular trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail is circular, some riders find that riding it in a specific direction can make certain challenging sections more manageable. However, the provided information does not specify a universally easier direction. Given the 'difficult' rating, be prepared for technical sections regardless of your chosen direction. Always assess the trail conditions and your own skill level.

waypoints.name: Nice views of the bay of the port of Sóller
